generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components //
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, converting electrical energy (input current I_M) to mechanical torque (τ_M) with maximum angular velocity of 75°/s."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component in the first axis transmission system, transmitting rotational motion from the motor to the cycloidal gearbox with defined gear ratio."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision cycloidal gearbox in the first axis transmission system providing torque amplification and speed reduction for the MABI MAX 100 robot's machining operations."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor, providing real-time rotational position data (r_M) for motion control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the cycloidal gearbox, providing output angle data (r_G) to enable backlash compensation and positional accuracy verification."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring the input current (I_M) supplied to the permanent magnet synchronous motor for torque calculation and control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InputCurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance InputCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLAxisBlockDefinition : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural composition of the first axis (Motor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox) and their relationships."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) defining the flow definitions (rotational, current) between components in the first axis transmission system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxSimulinkModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal gearbox behavior without friction, using manufacturer-provided parameters for dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model for simulating friction losses in the gearbox transmission system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model accounting for stiction and breakaway behavior in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model capturing dynamic friction characteristics in the transmission system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model providing detailed simulation of friction phenomena in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotorGearboxDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating motor and gearbox dynamics using manufacturer parameters for torque-speed characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredInputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of measured input current (I_M) collected from the physical system via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of measured motor output angle (r_M) from the primary encoder, used for motion analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance MeasuredGearWheelOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data trace of measured gearwheel output angle (r_G) from the secondary encoder, accounting for gearbox backlash."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque data (τ_M) simulated from friction models and manufacturer parameters for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearboxTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque data (τ_G) simulated through the gearbox transmission system accounting for friction losses."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedSystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived system output angle (r_out) simulated from combined motor and gearbox dynamics with friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-based modeling environment enabling structural and behavioral modeling of the robot axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, SimulationValidationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink platform executing executable friction models and dynamics simulations for the gearbox system."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, SimulationValidationService, ControlOptimizationService
]

instance MQTTDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling real-time data streaming from physical sensors to the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized AAS framework providing asset description and data traceability for the first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService, DataVisualizationService
]

instance FrictionAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom processing component implementing friction model calculations (white-box/black-box) for torque analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dynamic model exchange system enabling real-time updates of friction models to refine control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlOptimizationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard platform integrating measured vs. simulated data traces for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous identification of joint friction (static, kinetic, viscous) using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models to improve motion accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:provides StaticFrictionInsight, KineticFrictionInsight, ViscousFrictionInsight
]

instance SimulationValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comparison of simulated data (from MATLAB Simulink models) against measured data to detect deviations caused by friction."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueDeviationInsight, VelocityDeviationInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of data traces into a unified dashboard for real-time monitoring of torque (τ), angular velocity (ω), and system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic exchange of friction models to refine control algorithms for improved robot precision in machining operations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlParameterUpdateAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StaticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Characterization of static friction components in the gearbox transmission system enabling targeted model improvements."
]

instance KineticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of kinetic friction characteristics affecting motion accuracy during gearbox operation."
]

instance ViscousFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of viscous friction effects in the transmission system impacting system response and energy efficiency."
]

instance TorqueDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of torque deviations between simulated and measured data indicating friction-related losses in the gearbox."
]

instance VelocityDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of angular velocity discrepancies caused by unmodeled friction phenomena in the transmission system."
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous visualization of torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) data traces enabling real-time operational monitoring."
]

instance ControlParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic update of control parameters in the Siemens SINUMERIK controller based on refined friction models."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Data Transmitted (Additional)
instance SimulatedDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

// C13: Environment/Component
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot with angular range [-90°, 90°]."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder, InputCurrentSensor
]

instance FrictionShadow : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM) encapsulating all friction-related models, data traces, and processing components for the first axis."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time-scale operates in **real-time** with **high-frequency data acquisition** (exact frequency not specified but implied by MQTT streaming and sensor updates). The digital shadow continuously processes **live measurements** (e.g., encoder data, current) and **simulation outputs** to update friction models dynamically. Synchronization between PT and DS occurs at the granularity of **individual data points** (torque/velocity samples) within the operational cycle of the robot axis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as a **purpose-driven digital shadow** with the following orchestration:
  1. **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) and derived data (torque/velocity calculations).
  2. **Processing Layer**: Simulation models (Simulink) and data calculation models (friction models) that consume input traces (*I_M*, *r_M*) and produce output traces (*τ̂_G*, *τ̂_out*).
  3. **Model Integration**: SysML structural models (asset description) and simulation models (behavioral analysis) are linked via **traceability associations** to ensure semantic consistency.
  4. **Visualization**: A **unified dashboard** aggregates all data traces (measured vs. simulated) for real-time analysis.
  The system follows a **one-way data flow** (physical → digital) with no feedback to the PT, adhering to the **digital shadow reference model** (DSRM)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation (real-time monitoring and adaptation). It does not cover design or manufacturing phases but instead **continuously refines friction models** based on runtime data to improve motion accuracy. The DS is **purpose-driven**, targeting **real-time friction detection** and **control optimization** during active use."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Comparison of **simulated data** (from Simulink) against **measured data** to quantify friction-induced deviations.
  - **Uncertainty Analysis**: Use of **multiple friction models** (white-box and black-box) to cross-validate results and reduce parameter dependency.
  - **Traceability**: Linking data traces to their **sources** (sensors, models) and **processing components** (Simulink blocks) ensures reproducibility.
  - **Verification**: Manufacturer-provided parameters and **sensor calibration** (e.g., encoder accuracy) underpin data fidelity. The DS does not include **closed-loop validation** (no feedback to PT), relying instead on **offline analysis** for model improvement."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between PT and DT involves:
  - **Data Protocols**: **MQTT** for **real-time streaming** of sensor data (*I_M*, encoder readings) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to the DS.
  - **Network Architecture**: **Cyber-physical system (CPS) integration** where sensors act as **data sources** and Simulink models as **processing nodes**.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data traces stored as **arrays of floating-point values** (torque *τ*, angular velocity *ω*) with **metadata** (source, timestamp, model parameters).
  - **Latency**: Implicitly low-latency due to real-time sensor updates and Simulink’s execution environment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted in a **software environment** integrating:
  - **SysML Models**: Stored in a **modeling tool** (e.g., IBM Rational Rhapsody or similar) for structural/behavioral description.
  - **Simulation Environment**: MATLAB Simulink for **executable friction models** and dynamics simulations.
  - **Data Storage**: **Time-series databases** (not explicitly named) for storing data traces (*I_M*, *r_M*, etc.).
  - **Visualization Platform**: A **unified dashboard** (likely web-based) for real-time monitoring and analysis. The deployment follows a **hybrid approach**, combining **offline simulation** (for model development) and **online processing** (for live data analysis)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: For **structural and behavioral modeling** of the robot axis.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Standardized framework for **asset description** and **data traceability** (per IEC 62422).
  - **MQTT Protocol**: Industry-standard for **real-time data streaming** in IoT/CPS applications.
  - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)**: Methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** for system abstraction.
  The DS follows the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** as defined in [15], ensuring interoperability with other MBSE-compliant tools."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
  - **Data Integrity**: MQTT ensures **encrypted data transmission** (implied by industrial IoT standards) to prevent tampering.
  - **Access Control**: The DS is designed as a **passive entity** (no autonomous actions), reducing risks of unauthorized PT manipulation.
  - **Safety Cell Integration**: The robot operates within a **restricted workspace** ([−90°, 90°]) and **speed limits** (75°/s), mitigating collision risks.
  - **No Remote Operation**: The DS does not execute **remote commands** on the PT, adhering to the **digital shadow’s one-way data flow** principle. Safety is ensured by the **CNC controller’s closed-loop control** (Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl.), which remains the authoritative system for motion execution."
]
